item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: SCP-1481, the Crack Genie?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Could it be linked to another genie or Arabian SCiP instead? I thought we had another genie around here somewhere.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I dig it.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. So given the fact that this is a toy, and SCP-2795 exists and serves a purpose quite similar to SCP-2654 with respect to helping people out, its possible the SCPF would look into whether this is yet another leaked Dr. Wondertainment original. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Once SCP-2654-1 has been given the food it has requested, it will remind the subject of the question they asked SCP-2654 and give them a random answer from SCP-2654's bank of responses. Once SCP-2654-1 has finished its requested food, it will disappear.

To this:

Once SCP-2654-1 has been given the food it has requested, it will remind the subject of the question they asked SCP-2654 and give them a random answer from SCP-2654's bank of responses. Once SCP-2654-1 has finished its requested food, it will disappear.1

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Given SCP-2654's similarity to SCP-2795, and toy based nature, an investigation into possible association with Dr. Wondertainment has been initiated. Currently, no link has been established between SCP-2654 and the GoI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the concept but it feels incomplete, I was left wanting more - an interview log would be nice, or addenda or test logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't feel like a complete article. There's just not enough here to go on, and I sure as heck didn't put together the explanation in the first post from what's in the article. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it will remind the subject of the question they asked SCP-2654 and give them a random answer from SCP-2654's bank of responses.

That was a tough question. I really had to think about which random and inaccurate response I should give for that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a fondness for SCPs that don't kill people. This manages to be realistically creepy in a completely non-lethal way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is eerily similar to a character from the DnD game we're both in, and that makes it even better.

Short, to the point, and just crazy enough to make me grin. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is really calling out for an interview log. +1, but I don't feel it's memorable without an interview log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty much how I imagined it, and why I was able to heartily +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some people need to consider:

The image of a naked hairless blue guy gently waking you up at 3 am, asking if he could have some Goldfish crackers and perhaps milk.

Groggily getting up and not comprehending the situation, you oblige, and provide the blue dude with his food. Both of you are sitting at your home's kitchen table. 2654-1 is quietly munching on his crackers, and you're half-awake staring at the guy.

"Thanks for the food. Oh, and uh, you know that question you asked a week ago? About you getting a promotion? Magic 8 ball?

"mm i guess what about it"

"My sources say yes. Congrats."

*yawn* "wowwee, cool thanks"

17 seconds of silence and crunching.

"Goldfish are so good, man. Snack that smiles back, am I right?"

"who are you and why are you in my home"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, short and fun to read. While keeping a sense of mystery. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems cool, I like it. A bit clunky in the description, a bit clunky overall, but good idea and decently implemented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How are they certain that the answer is randomly determined?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In context, that comes across more as something discovered through testing than implying a standard procedure of luxury for the SCP. The containment procedures say they only keep a reserve of junk food for unexpected manifestations. Otherwise its locked up tight. Disagreeing with how the Foundation would operate is also not the same thing as tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Generally when I write, I write so it can be easily understood by anyone who reads it. I don't like falling into Sesquipedalian Loquaciousness, as I feel that makes it harder for the reader to enjoy the piece as a creative work. I am trying, and failing, to figure how those are bad tone. They're succinct and get the point across without any subjectivity or emotion. Unless you can offer me better suggestions, I'm going to leave them

And to answer your previous question about containing -1. He disappears as soon as he provides an answer, there's not really anything the Foundation could do to catch him, but he's linked to the magic eight ball, so it's not like he's going anywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think my favorite part here is that it doesn't give accurate answers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once SCP-2654-1 has been given the food it has requested, it will remind the subject of the question they asked SCP-2654 and give them a random answer from SCP-2654's bank of responses. Once SCP-2654-1 has finished its requested food, it will disappear.

This is the biggest offender to my eyes. Given the food, requested food, finished, bank of responses - all of these feel way too breezy for the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first and only thought is, "I'm Mr. Eight-Ball, look at me!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The tone is fine by my reading, it sounds authentic enough. You don't make any specific notes of where the tone felt off which makes the critique kind of difficult to apply constructively.

Comment on the article: I think this is a neat little package of weirdness served in just the right portion to keep it feeling like a cohesive whole without anything extra. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted, as there's significant problems with tone and an uncompelling/poorly conveyed high concept. Additionally, why not try to contain 2654-1?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
/me shakes 8-ball

"GOOD WEIRD SHIT DONE WELL WITH A GOOD KIND OF SERIES 1 VIBE WOULD UPVOTE"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was sitting in my sandbox for a few months. Thank you to Roget for giving it a quick read before posting.

I was aiming for a sort of Series 1 vibe with this.

E. Since a few people have asked, the backstory for this SCP is that SCP-2654-1 is a Djinn, who, during the cold war, moved to America and choose a Magic Eight ball as his vessel. He found he prefers American food to Arabic, so he stuck around.
